frameworks.name: resource-rational sequential evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: normative trade-off between estimation accuracy and encoding/memory cost

frameworks.name: bump attractor network
frameworks.one_line: neural integration account that fails to capture break dynamics

frameworks.name: temporal integration of sensory evidence
frameworks.one_line: perceptual decisions integrate samples over time

core_question: How does a temporal break in a sample sequence reshape the weighting of post-break evidence in perceptual estimation?
key_assumptions: samples are drawn from a Gaussian generative mean with fixed variance
observer actively manages a trade-off between accuracy and encoding/memory effort
manipulations: presence and duration of a mid-sequence break
position of the break (after 2nd, 4th, or 6th sample)
key_findings: weight on first post-break sample increases monotonically with break duration
effect varies continuously with break duration rather than thresholded
normative resource-rational model fits data across break and no-break conditions
interpretations: post-break upweighting reflects continuous resource-rational reallocation, not a threshold reset
bump-attractor accounts cannot capture the observed integration dynamics
